fsm_model.description: Secure synchronization of message counters for group encrypted messages to prevent replay attacks

roles.description: Node that receives unsynchronized message and initiates counter synchronization
Node that responds to synchronization requests with current counter value

states.description: Node is idle, no active synchronization exchange
invariants: No active MCSP exchange for this peer and group key pair
RemainingTime == 0 or undefined
No cached messages waiting for synchronization

states.description: Receiver has received a message from peer whose counter is unknown
invariants: Message is cryptographically verified
Message is of Group Session Type
No Message Reception State exists for this peer and group key
C Flag determines trust-first vs cache-and-sync handling
GroupKeySecurityPolicy == cache-and-sync
Unsynchronized message is stored for later processing
No other MCSP exchange active for this peer and group key
Random jitter delay may be applied before sending request

states.description: Initiator has sent MsgCounterSyncReq and awaits MsgCounterSyncRsp
invariants: Active synchronization exchange exists
Challenge value stored for response verification
Timer armed with MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT
Exchange ID, Source and Destination Node IDs recorded
Session context maintained (Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Role, etc.)

states.description: Responder received MsgCounterSyncReq and is preparing response
invariants: Destination Node ID matches receiver's Node ID
MsgCounterSyncReq is authenticated and decrypted
Challenge value extracted from request
Session context created for response (maintained only during response generation)
Peer's group key data message counter set to Synchronized Counter
Message Reception State bitmap cleared for the peer
Peer's counter marked as synchronized
Cached message(s) processed in order received
Synchronization exchange closed

states.description: Synchronization response not received before timeout
invariants: MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT expired
Synchronization exchange closed
Any cached message waiting on this exchange discarded
Peer remains unsynchronized
Response silently ignored
One or more verification checks failed (Exchange ID, Challenge/Response, Node IDs)
Peer remains unsynchronized
Exchange remains active until timeout

guard: MessageReceptionState does not exist for message.SourceNodeID and GroupKey
actions: Extract SourceNodeID from message
Verify message authentication
Determine if control (C Flag = 1) or data (C Flag = 0) message
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate upon message receipt and authentication

actions: Create Message Reception State
Set max_message_counter := message.MessageCounter
Clear Message Reception State bitmap
Mark peer's counter as synchronized
Process the message immediately
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate trust-first synchronization without MCSP exchange

guard: No active MCSP exchange for this peer Node ID and group key
actions: Store message for later processing
Record Peer Node ID from message.SourceNodeID
Record Group Session ID and operational group key
Initiate random jitter delay between 0 and MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER
transitions.timing_requirements: Message cached immediately; jitter delay before sending request

guard: MCSP already in progress for this peer Node ID and group key
actions: Do not process the message
Implementation MAY queue message if resources allow
transitions.timing_requirements: At most one synchronization exchange per peer-key pair at a time

guard: No active MCSP exchange for this peer and group key
actions: Create new synchronization Exchange
Set Message Header: S Flag := 1, DSIZ := 1, P Flag := 1, C Flag := 1, Session Type := 1
Set Session ID := Group Session Id for the operational group key
Set Source Node ID := sender's Node ID
Set Destination Node ID := peer's Node ID (from cached message)
Set Exchange ID to new exchange, I Flag := 1, A Flag := 0, R Flag := 1
Challenge := Crypto_DRBG(len = 64 bits)
Store Challenge for response verification
Arm timer := MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT
Create session context (Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Role := Initiator, Message Reception State, Peer IP/Port, Operational Group Key, Group Session ID)
Encrypt and send MsgCounterSyncReq via Section 4.7.1 to peer's unicast IPv6 address
transitions.timing_requirements: Request sent after jitter delay via unicast UDP

guard: Message is authenticated and decrypted with group key
actions: Extract Challenge from payload
Record Source Node ID (initiator)
Record Exchange ID, Session ID, Group Key
Create temporary session context for response (Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Role := Responder, Message Reception State, Peer IP/Port, Operational Group Key, Group Session ID)
Set Message Header: S Flag := 1, DSIZ := 1, P Flag := 1, C Flag := 1, Session Type := 1
Set Session ID := Group Session Id from request
Set Source Node ID := responder's Node ID
Set Destination Node ID := initiator's Node ID (from request)
Set Exchange ID := request.ExchangeID, I Flag := 0, A Flag := 1, R Flag := 1
Set Response := Challenge (from request)
Set Synchronized Counter := current Global Group Encrypted Data Message Counter
Encrypt and send MsgCounterSyncRsp via Section 4.7.1 to initiator's unicast IPv6 address
Discard session context after successful transmission
transitions.timing_requirements: Response sent immediately via unicast UDP; session context released after transmission

guard: Active synchronization exchange exists with source node
message.SourceNodeID == peer's Node ID (Destination of request)
actions: Stop MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT timer
Set peer's group key data message counter := message.SynchronizedCounter
Clear Message Reception State bitmap for the peer (Section 4.6.5.1)
Mark peer's group key data message counter as synchronized
Process cached message(s) according to Section 4.6.7
If multiple messages queued from same peer with same key, process in order received
Close synchronization exchange
transitions.timing_requirements: Counter updated and cached messages processed upon successful verification

guard: One or more verification checks fail:
No active exchange with source node
actions: Silently ignore the MsgCounterSyncRsp message
Do not update any peer state
Timer remains armed
transitions.timing_requirements: Invalid response discarded; no state change

actions: Close synchronization exchange
Discard any cached message waiting on this exchange
Peer remains unsynchronized
Clear session context
transitions.timing_requirements: Timeout enforces bounded wait for response

guard: MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT timer fired after verification failure
actions: Close synchronization exchange
Discard any cached message
Clear session context
transitions.timing_requirements: Exchange terminates on timeout even after failed verification

actions: Return to idle state
Peer is now synchronized for future messages
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate transition after message processing completes

actions: Return to idle state
Ready for new synchronization attempts
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate return to idle after timeout cleanup

fields.description: Generated using DRBG to uniquely identify synchronization request cryptographically

header_requirements: Control message counter used for protection
Secured with group key for which synchronization is requested
I Flag := 1, A Flag := 0, R Flag := 1

fields.description: Current data message counter for the node sending response
Echo of Challenge from corresponding MsgCounterSyncReq

header_requirements: Control message counter used for protection
I Flag := 0, A Flag := 1, R Flag := 1

constants.description: Maximum random delay before sending sync request (uniformly random 0 to this value)
constants.usage: Prevents thundering herd when multiple receivers get same multicast message

constants.description: Maximum time to wait for MsgCounterSyncRsp before giving up
Size of bitmap tracking recent message counter window
constants.usage: Used in Message Reception State for duplicate detection

security_properties.description: MCSP prevents replay attacks by ensuring receiver knows sender's current message counter
security_properties.formal_statement: After successful synchronization, any message with counter <= max_message_counter (outside acceptance window) SHALL be rejected as duplicate
security_properties.mitigation: Cache-and-sync policy ensures counter synchronization before processing first message, even after reboot

security_properties.description: MsgCounterSyncReq and MsgCounterSyncRsp are authenticated using group key
security_properties.formal_statement: Only nodes possessing valid operational group key can successfully send or verify synchronization messages
security_properties.mitigation: Both request and response use C Flag = 1 with group key encryption and control message counter

security_properties.formal_statement: Response.Challenge == Request.Challenge AND Response.ExchangeID == Request.ExchangeID ensures response corresponds to current request
security_properties.mitigation: 64-bit random Challenge from DRBG makes replay of old responses infeasible

security_properties.description: Counter values transmitted in encrypted messages
security_properties.formal_statement: Synchronized Counter is transmitted in encrypted MsgCounterSyncRsp, preventing passive eavesdropper from learning counter state
security_properties.mitigation: Group key encryption of both request and response messages

security_properties.description: Message integrity ensured by encryption and authentication
security_properties.formal_statement: Any modification to MsgCounterSyncReq or MsgCounterSyncRsp results in authentication failure
security_properties.mitigation: Group key provides both encryption and message integrity check

security_properties.description: At most one synchronization exchange per peer-key pair at a time
security_properties.formal_statement: While MCSP in progress for peer P and key K, additional unsynchronized messages from P with K are either queued or discarded
security_properties.mitigation: Prevents resource exhaustion from synchronization flood; timeout ensures bounded resource commitment

security_properties.description: Trust-first policy susceptible to replay after reboot
security_properties.formal_statement: WARNING: Trust-first synchronization may accept replayed message after node reboot if Message Reception State not persisted
security_properties.mitigation: Cache-and-sync policy recommended for security-sensitive applications; control messages always use trust-first with higher risk acceptance

security_properties.description: Response bound to specific request initiator
security_properties.formal_statement: MsgCounterSyncRsp.DestinationNodeID must match initiator's Node ID; response only accepted by original requester
security_properties.mitigation: Node ID verification in response processing prevents misdirection attacks

security_properties.description: Each MCSP exchange isolated by Exchange ID
security_properties.formal_statement: Exchange ID uniquely identifies request-response pair; responses with mismatched Exchange ID silently discarded
security_properties.mitigation: Prevents cross-exchange confusion and response injection

security_properties.formal_statement: Session context includes Fabric Index; synchronization only valid within identified fabric
security_properties.mitigation: Operational group key tied to fabric; encryption ensures fabric isolation

references.title: Message Reception State
references.description: Defines bitmap-based tracking of received message counters for duplicate detection

references.title: Counter Processing of Incoming Messages
references.description: Specifies how to determine Message Reception State and handle unsynchronized messages

references.title: Message Transmission
references.description: Defines encryption and transmission procedure used for both MsgCounterSyncReq and MsgCounterSyncRsp

references.title: Group Key Management Cluster
references.description: Defines GroupKeySecurityPolicy (trust-first vs cache-and-sync) and FeatureMap indicating MCSP support

references.title: Group Peer State
references.description: Defines Group Peer State Table storing synchronized counters and message reception state per peer
